q: A row-major (M, N) fp32 tensor: what is the stride (in elements) between vertically adjacent elements, i.e. (i, j) and (i+1, j)?
why: Row-major stores each row contiguously, so moving down one row skips a whole row of N elements. This is why column-wise access is uncoalesced.

q: Why does softmax subtract the row max before exponentiating?
a: exp(x) overflows fp32 around x ~ 88; subtracting the max makes the largest argument 0, so every exp is in (0, 1].
why: The result is mathematically identical (the shift cancels in the ratio) but numerically safe.

q: Vector add on N fp32 elements moves how many bytes total (loads + stores)?
a: 12N bytes: read a (4N), read b (4N), write out (4N).
why: Memory-bound kernels are judged on bytes moved / time; knowing the traffic is step one of any bandwidth estimate.

q: In Triton, what does tl.program_id(axis=0) identify, and what is the classic bug if the last program's block runs past the end of the data?
a: The index of this program instance in the launch grid; without a mask the last block does out-of-bounds loads/stores.
why: Grid = ceil(N / BLOCK_SIZE) programs, so the tail block is partially valid -- every load/store needs the offsets < n_elements mask.
